seoTitle: How to Collect Payments Using Typeform
metaDescription: Learn how to set up a payment form in Typeform, connect a payment provider, configure pricing, and test checkout before publishing.
intro: Accepting payments through a form can simplify selling products, services, or event tickets without a full checkout page. This guide covers how to set up payment collection in Typeform, connect a supported payment provider, and test everything before sharing the form with customers.

steps.heading: Create a payment form
steps.body: Start a new Typeform designed to collect payment, adding the fields needed to describe what's being purchased.

steps.heading: Connect payment provider
steps.body: Link a supported payment provider to your Typeform account so transactions can be processed securely.

steps.heading: Configure pricing and checkout
steps.body: Set prices for products or services and customize the checkout experience so it's clear what respondents are paying for.

steps.heading: Test and publish your form
steps.body: Run a test transaction to confirm payments process correctly, then publish the form and share it with customers.

faq.question: What kind of payments can I collect with Typeform?
faq.answer: Typeform supports collecting payments for products, services, event registrations, consultations, and donations through connected payment providers.

faq.question: Is it safe to collect payments through a Typeform?
faq.answer: Typeform relies on supported payment providers to process transactions securely, so payment handling follows those providers' security standards.

faq.question: Can I test the payment form before making it live?
faq.answer: Yes, it's recommended to test your payment form to confirm pricing and checkout work correctly before sharing it with customers.

faq.question: Do I need a specific payment provider to use this feature?
faq.answer: You need to connect one of Typeform's supported payment providers to enable payment collection on your form.
